sources.strategy_deck: /Users/joy/Downloads/M2P Finflux CLS FY27 Strategy Deck v1.0.pdf

data_quality_notes: The workbook row-level loss sum does not equal the 100.5 Cr headline from the deck/report.
Roadmap totals vary across deck, report, roadmap PDF, and workbook line-item sums.
The command center should surface these as versioned planning numbers, not silently collapse them into one truth.

1: Prioritize Gold Loan + LAS/LAMF in Q1 without compromise
2: Staff Capital Markets (CAP-01/02) and Islamic Finance (ISL-01/02) with domain experts in Q2 preparation
3: Resolve DPDPA and audit module by Q2 to unblock large bank pipeline
4: Invest in SI-Ready Framework to multiply implementation throughput
5: Treat AI Moat as a pricing tool, not just a feature

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Core-Agnostic Spine — Event-Driven, API-First Architecture
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Vivamoney ₹8 Cr (build-vs-buy)
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Single biggest objection killer: removes 'too hard to integrate' veto
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Ongoing foundational work; block zero for LOS/LMS/BRE orchestration
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Platform durability — every new product & market depends on this spine

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Config Platform 2.0 — Product Setup, Schedules, NPA Rules, KFS
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Demo friction costing early-stage deals
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Reduces POC-to-pilot time from weeks to days
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Eliminates most pre-sales objections around setup complexity
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Shortens sales cycles; directly improves win-rate optics

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: UAT→Prod Config Replication + Config Hygiene
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Go-live credibility gap vs Newgen/Pennant
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Turns implementations into referenceable go-lives faster
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Automates config promotion; reduces go-live errors
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Reduces churn risk post-sale; improves NPS and references

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: BNPL Credit Line Parity — Pre-EMI, Moratoriums, Flexible RPS
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): BNPL pipeline losses; Cashalo, Velocity, PayU WIP
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Compete head-on in modern BNPL/credit-line RFPs
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Fills installment definition gaps, pre-EMI, moratorium flows
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: BNPL TAM growing 35% YoY; 71% parity today is a deal-killer

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: LAP 2.0 — Collateral Logic, Sale-of-Asset, Richer Lifecycle Flows
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Kissht ₹3.5 Cr, InPrime ₹3 Cr lost on feature gap
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Compete head-on in high-ticket LAP/mortgage RFPs
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Adds sale-of-asset workflows, richer collateral logic
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: ₹6.5 Cr direct loss; LAP ticket sizes are 5-10x PL

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Home Loan 1.0 — Dedicated Product, Lifecycle & Repayment Logic
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Saarthi Finance ₹3 Cr lost on feature gap
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Enter core home-loan market without PL/LAP workarounds
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Dedicated HL product setup with full repayment lifecycle

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: LAS/LAMF 1.0 — Collateral Engine, Exposure Controls, Market Feeds
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Neo Finance ₹5 Cr lost to Nucleus; LAMF parity only 53%
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Unlock wealthtech & capital-markets NBFC conversations
roadmap_master.PM Notes: NSDL/CDSL integration, LTV engine, pledge/unpledge workflows
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: ₹5 Cr direct loss; LAMF TAM growing with wealth management boom

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Gold Loan Critical — Vault Ops, Ornament Controls, Auction Readiness
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Piramal ₹5 Cr, L&T ₹6 Cr, ABCL ₹5 Cr lost to Pennant; Gold parity only 20%
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Become a credible contender for gold-heavy lenders
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Real-time ornament handling, vault/inventory, auction readiness
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: ₹16 Cr direct loss; gold NBFC market growing with new NBFC entrants

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: SCF 1.0 — Anchor/Dealer/Vendor Disbursement & Reporting
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Credflow ₹3 Cr lost on product gap
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Participate in supply-chain RFPs; cross-sell to PL/BL base
roadmap_master.PM Notes: First SCF offering: anchor-dealer-vendor flows, disbursements

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Auction & Repo MVP — Events, Approvals, Accounting

roadmap_master.PM Notes: End-to-end auction and repossession with GL accounting

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Co-lending Middleware — Onboarding, Splits, Triple Accounting

roadmap_master.PM Notes: Smart recommendations and anomaly surfacing on portfolio

roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Cannot sell ISL-01 without this; certification is gating requirement

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: TDS on Interest & Fees — Auto Computation, Posting, Reporting
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Finance team migration confidence at risk without TDS automation
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Automate TDS and remove major finance ops pain point
roadmap_master.PM Notes: TDS computation, posting, 26AS reconciliation, eTDS filing

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Bank Reconciliation Auto/Semi-Auto — Rule-Driven, 4-Annexure Reports
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): CFO-facing differentiator for MCA/B2B portfolios
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Deliver a CFO-grade efficiency lever for MCA/B2B demos
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Auto/semi-auto bank recon with LMS entry matching
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Bank recon is CFO's biggest pain; this becomes a key demo moment

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: B2B/MCA Merchant Risk, Covenant Tracking, Seasonal Limits
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Targets high-ACV SME/MCA RFPs where covenant tracking is mandatory
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Win structured SME/B2B lending with covenant-ready platform
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Covenant definition, tracking, alerts, seasonal limit engine
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: B2B lending programs = highest ACV per deal in our TAM

roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): International deals (GCC, SE Asia) blocked without data residency
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Unblock international pipeline that currently stalls at InfoSec
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Market-specific data residency, regulatory extracts per jurisdiction

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: AI Moat 3.0 — Covenant Risk, Collection Prioritisation, B2B Insights
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): AI story in B2B portfolios differentiates from legacy LMS vendors
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Turn operational data into actionable B2B portfolio insights
roadmap_master.PM Notes: AI-driven covenant risk scoring and collection prioritisation
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: B2B clients pay premium for intelligence layer vs raw transaction engine

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Bug-a-thon 2.0 — Stabilise Expanded Feature Surface
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Stabilise B2B and vehicle finance surface before major go-lives
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Stabilize the expanded B2B surface and protect brand
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Cross-functional bug-a-thon for new B2B and vehicle modules
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Quality at scale is a brand promise, not just a tech task

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Env-as-Code, Observability, SLOs
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Bank IT due diligence readiness for enterprise evaluations
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Pass bank IT due diligence SLA/observability requirements
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Infrastructure as code, SLO dashboards, alert management
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Table-stakes for bank partnerships; non-optional by FY28

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Microfinance Advanced Pack (SEG-01b) — Loan Utilization Checks, Collection Enhancements, MFI Product Config, GL Maintenance, Deceased Member & Claims Processing, IRACP/IRAAC Provisioning
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Avanti, Svamaan WIP pipeline; MFI co-lending mandates requiring full lifecycle coverage; Craft Silicon displacement requires completeness
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Deliver the second half of the MFI lifecycle to win against Craft Silicon: loan utilization evidence capture with geo-tagging, deceased member claims with full insurance accounting, and IRACP provisioning — the three capabilities that regulators and co-lending banks specifically audit
roadmap_master.PM Notes: SEG-01 (Q2) delivers Branch/Center/SOD-COB-EOD/Commission. SEG-01b (Q3) adds the remaining 6 of 11 MFI requirements: Loan Utilization Checks, Collection Enhancements (pre-closure + write-off recovery), MFI Product Configuration (NPA auto-move, net-off rules, settlement allocation), GL Maintenance (BS/OBS hierarchy, period close, immutable audit), Deceased Member & Claims Processing (full claims lifecycle + premium accounting), and IRACP/IRAAC provisioning (DPD bucket rules, incremental + full provisioning, sub-ledger recon). PD to be refined in Q2 planning.
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Full MFI certification against Craft Silicon requires both SEG-01 and SEG-01b. Partial delivery stalls co-lending bank onboarding that requires IRACP provisioning and PSL-linked audit evidence. Together they represent a complete, referenceable MFI platform — the only segment where Finflux can displace an entrenched specialist vendor through architecture and cloud-native design advantages.

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Loan Against Insurance — Policy as Collateral, LTV Engine, Insurer Events

roadmap_master.PM Notes: CDC event streams for bank ML pipeline integration
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Gating requirement for enterprise bank partnerships from FY28

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Real-Time Portfolio Guardrails — Kill-Switches, Intraday Caps
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Large sophisticated NBFCs need intraday risk controls
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Win sophisticated NBFCs with real-time portfolio risk controls
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Configurable kill-switches, intraday caps, breach alerts
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Table-stakes for Tier-1 NBFC with >₹5,000 Cr AUM

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Implementation Cockpit — Config Delta Tracking, Cutover Readiness
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): SI ecosystem scaling requires implementation tooling
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Accelerate SI partner-led implementations at scale
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Config delta tracking, cutover checklists, readiness scoring
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Multiplies implementation throughput as SI ecosystem grows

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: ESG & Climate Tags — Asset Labelling, Green Loan Flags
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): International/regulatory RFPs citing ESG by FY28
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Future-proof the platform for ESG-mandated RFPs
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Green loan labelling, climate risk tagging per ISSB
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: IFC/DFI-linked lenders need this; mandated by FY28 internationally

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Bug Bash 2.0 — Full Sprint Ecosystem-Wide Stability
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Platform stability for rapid ecosystem onboarding in FY27 H2
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Ensure platform-wide stability for ecosystem onboarding
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Sprint dedicated to system-wide regression and ecosystem surface
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Stable platform = faster partner onboarding = compounding revenue

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Lease Product Engine — Operating Lease, Finance Lease, Residual Value & Buyback Accounting
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): Drivn ₹3 Cr lost to Qualtech on leasing product readiness; leasing pipeline in Philippines & SEA
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Win leasing mandates by covering operating lease, finance lease classification, residual value LTV, and buyback option at maturity — deal-breaker requirements Qualtech met and Finflux did not
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Dedicated lease product types with operating/finance classification, residual value engine, buyback handling, and Ind AS 116-aligned accounting; distinct from standard EMI loan accounting
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Leasing is a growing asset class across auto, equipment, and technology finance; absence of a proper lease engine is a category exclusion in any leasing-specific RFP

roadmap_master.EPIC / Initiative: Fleet Finance Pack — Multi-Asset Limits, Fleet-Level DPD Aggregation & OEM Program Structures
roadmap_master.Loss Linkage (\u20b9 Cr): TWL and fleet losses; Hinduja Leyland ₹4 Cr; fleet-specific limit and aggregation gaps not covered by PROD-06b
roadmap_master.Sales Angle: Address fleet programs where a single borrower operates multiple vehicles under one credit limit, with fleet-level delinquency tracking and OEM/manufacturer rebate program support
roadmap_master.PM Notes: Multi-asset tracking under a single borrower limit, fleet-level DPD and NPA aggregation, OEM rebate and subvention program structures, fleet utilisation and exposure dashboards
roadmap_master.C-Suite Rationale: Fleet programs are high-ACV, long-tenure relationships with commercial vehicle OEMs and large fleet operators; winning one fleet program typically anchors multi-year AUM growth

imperatives.title: Close the Revenue Bleed
imperatives.body: ₹100.5+ Cr lost in FY26. The source docs repeatedly concentrate the fix on Gold (20% parity), LAMF/LAS (53%), BNPL/Credit Line (71%), and Vehicle Finance. Q1 carries 16 workbook EPICs to recover the highest-confidence lost revenue first.

imperatives.title: Pass the Compliance Firewall
imperatives.body: DPDPA, ECL/EIR, Audit Module 2.0, and NPA automation show up as veto gates across bank and large NBFC deals. Q2 has 17 workbook EPICs, with compliance-heavy scope positioned to remove those gates before larger enterprise pushes.

imperatives.title: Enter Two New High-Value Markets
imperatives.body: Islamic Finance for GCC/ASEAN and Capital Markets/B2B are the clearest net-new FY27 bets in the source docs. Q3 concentrates 18 workbook EPICs into those wedges instead of treating them as side quests.

imperatives.title: Accelerate Delivery with AI Tooling
imperatives.body: The source narrative treats AI twice: internal engineering leverage and external product monetization. The current executive baseline assumes 1,100-1,400 PD of engineering recovery plus 15-25% AI-linked ACV uplift when packaged correctly.

imperatives.title: Build the Loss-Prevention Framework
imperatives.body: The report does not frame FY26 losses as feature-only. Trust deficit, weak references, implementation friction, and poor qualification all recur. Hypercare, QBRs, tighter qualification, and win-loss loops are therefore operating requirements, not GTM decoration.

moats.title: Islamic Finance / GCC
moats.body: The strategy deck calls this the only category where Finflux can plausibly create first-mover advantage in FY27: cloud-native Islamic LMS capability for GCC/ASEAN with Shariah governance, not just conventional lending relabeled.

moats.title: AI-Native LMS Intelligence
moats.body: The moat is not generic AI. It comes from portfolio, repayment, collections, and covenant signals already living inside LMS workflows. The source docs connect that to both internal leverage and premium ACV expansion.

moats.title: Co-lending Orchestration Hub
moats.body: Co-lending is treated as infrastructure, not one feature. Middleware, triple-book accounting, partner onboarding, and repeatable integrations create a distribution and delivery moat if Q1 execution is credible enough to become referenceable.

friction_zones.label: 01 Product
friction_zones.title: Product Maturity & Gaps
friction_zones.body: The sources repeatedly position Finflux as evaluation-worthy but not enterprise-complete. Gold, LAMF, BNPL, compliance depth, and collections maturity are the most repeated examples.

friction_zones.label: 02 Engineering
friction_zones.title: Velocity & Innovation Dilemma
friction_zones.body: Escalations, stabilization work, and implementation drag absorb roadmap energy. The FY27 plan responds with bug-bash cycles, AI leverage, and sharper sequencing rather than pretending velocity is already solved.

friction_zones.label: 03 GTM
friction_zones.title: Sales Execution Misalignment
friction_zones.body: The strategy report is explicit that qualification, demo fidelity, and wishlist selling are contributing to losses. That means product truth and sales discipline need to tighten together.

friction_zones.label: 04 Ecosystem
friction_zones.title: Ecosystem Stagnation
friction_zones.body: Integration and implementation repeatability are still weak points. The plan treats SI readiness, co-lending infrastructure, and reusable integration assets as multipliers on product investment.

friction_zones.label: 05 LOS/BRE
friction_zones.title: LOS Foundation (WIP)
friction_zones.body: The source narrative keeps returning to orchestration quality: LOS, BRE, config, and integration spine are not optional plumbing. Weakness there degrades every downstream product category and implementation.

key_insight.label: Key Insight
key_insight.title: Completion Problem, Not Architecture
key_insight.body: The strategy report's 'Almost Comply' framing is the clearest synthesis: Finflux gets evaluated because the platform is credible, then loses the final 10-20% on completeness, compliance depth, and trust. FY27 is therefore a completion program, not a platform rewrite.

Executive Summary  Finflux enters FY 2026-27 with a critical inflection point. The platform has/had strong origination  capabilities and a growing high-integrity LMS core but is being systematically excluded from high-value  deals due to product parity gaps, compliance deficits, technical debts, maintenance traps, and an  absence of specialized asset-class coverage. FY26 losses totaling ₹100.5 Cr (understated) across 23  deals are not random — they map precisely to addressable product gaps. The good news: every one of  these losses has a corresponding EPIC in the FY27 roadmap.  The FY27 investment thesis rests on four pillars: first, closing the competitive gap in high -loss categories  (Gold, LAMF/LAS, Vehicle Finance) that cost ₹35+ Cr in FY26; second, achieving compliance parity  (DPDPA, ECL, EIR, TDS) that is blocking enterprise and regulated-lender deals; third, opening two new  hig

Market & Competitive Landscape  2.1  The Indian LMS Market is Stratifying  The Indian LMS vendor landscape is stratifying into three tiers. The first tier — Nucleus FinnOne,  Temenos, Pennant who dominates large bank and NBFC mandates through legacy client relationships,  deep feature coverage, and trusted brand equity. The second tier — M2P, Newgen, Craft Silicon,  Synoriq, A3SL — competes on cloud-native architecture, configurability, and price competitiveness. The  third tier — specialized point solutions (Lentra for LOS, Credgenics for collections, Yubi for co -lending,  Finbox for Embedded/MCA) — is eating into adjacent revenue streams. Recommendation: engage a capital markets SME consultant in Q2 to review and validate  accounting logic before go-live.  Islamic Finance (ISL-01/02): Shariah-compliant product accounting differs fundamentally from  conventional interest-based accounting. Murabaha cost-plus structures, Ijarah lease-to-own accounting,  and Musharakah diminishing partnership calculations require both domain expertise and Shariah board  sign-off. Finflux must engage a qualified Shariah advisor (ideally through a partnership with an  established Islamic finance consultancy in UAE or Malaysia) by Q1 to ensure the product design meets  certification requirements.
